notes: The four analog I/O connectors are DB25 (D-sub 25-pin) wired to the AES59 standard — this connector type is not in the schema; rs422 (also a D-sub family) is used as the closest physical match. Ethernet grade eth-1g is confirmed by the Neutrik NE8FBH connector spec (Gigabit-rated) and by Dante requirements at 16 channels/96kHz; Focusrite's spec page does not explicitly print the word Gigabit but the hardware confirms it. IEC inlets are C14 type with locking retaining clips.

ports.name: Line In 1-8
ports.note: Actual connector: DB25 (D-sub 25-pin), AES59 pinout, carries 8 channels of balanced analog line input. rs422 is the closest D-sub form factor in the schema.

ports.name: Line In 9-16
ports.note: Actual connector: DB25 (D-sub 25-pin), AES59 pinout, carries channels 9–16 of balanced analog line input.

ports.name: Line Out 1-8
ports.note: Actual connector: DB25 (D-sub 25-pin), AES59 pinout, carries 8 channels of balanced analog line output.

ports.name: Line Out 9-16
ports.note: Actual connector: DB25 (D-sub 25-pin), AES59 pinout, carries channels 9–16 of balanced analog line output.

ports.name: AES3 In
ports.note: Female XLR3; AES3/EBU digital stereo input; also accepts DARS; switchable with analogue channels 15–16 at quad sample rates.

ports.name: AES3 Out
ports.note: Male XLR3; AES3/EBU digital stereo output.

ports.name: Word Clock In
ports.note: 75Ω BNC word clock input with switchable termination.

ports.name: Network Primary
ports.note: Neutrik NE8FBH etherCON locking connector; Dante primary network; backward-compatible with standard RJ45.

ports.name: Network Secondary
ports.note: Neutrik NE8FBH etherCON locking connector; Dante secondary/redundant network; backward-compatible with standard RJ45.

ports.name: AC In 1
ports.note: IEC C14 inlet with retaining clip; PSU 1 of dual redundant power supplies.

ports.name: AC In 2
ports.note: IEC C14 inlet with retaining clip; PSU 2 of dual redundant power supplies.
